front: Film: Charles Foster Kane drops this object and whispers 'Rosebud' as he dies in the opening scene
Film: 'Rosebud' is revealed at the end to be this object, shown burning in a fireplace
Film: Kane's wife Susan Alexander performs in the opera Salammbo, which is panned; Kane finishes writing a review in the New York Inquirer
Film: a shrieking cockatoo punctuates a butler's memory of singer Susan Alexander
Film: opens with a three-minute-twenty-second tracking shot through a Mexican border town, ending with the explosion of a car bomb
Film: Welles plays the corrupt cop Hank Quinlan; the film ends with the lines 'He was some kind of man. What does it matter what you say about people?'
Film: cinematographer Russell Metty shot the famous opening tracking shot of this 1958 film
Film: the studio cut 40 minutes without Welles's approval while he was filming in Brazil; ends with Joseph Cotten providing for the fallen heir George
Film: Agnes Moorehead's character Fanny breaks down in the 'boiler room' scene after admitting she is nearly broke
Film: concludes with a shootout in a hall of mirrors at a funhouse; stars Welles's wife Rita Hayworth as femme fatale Elsa
Film: Welles plays Michael O'Hara, who agrees to fake the death of George Grisby before Elsa murders Grisby
Film: Harry Lime delivers the 'cuckoo clock' speech on a Ferris wheel, comparing Switzerland's 500 years of peace to producing only the cuckoo clock
Film: Harry Lime is revealed — thought dead — when a streetlamp lights a dark Vienna doorway; Anton Karas's zither score plays
Film: Welles flees through the sewers of Vienna in the climax of this Carol Reed noir
Director: his 1938 Halloween radio broadcast of War of the Worlds, staged by the Mercury Theatre on Air, allegedly caused mass public panic; newspapers exaggerated the hysteria to discredit radio
work: War of the Worlds Radio Broadcast (1938)

front: Director: the War of the Worlds broadcast landed an alien object in Grover's Mill, New Jersey; a repeat in Quito caused a mob to set fire to a building, killing a pianist and violinist
work: War of the Worlds Radio Broadcast (1938)

front: Director: directed the 'Voodoo Macbeth' for the Federal Theatre's New York Negro Unit, co-produced with John Houseman, featuring Eric Burroughs as a bullwhip-wielding Hecate
Director: directed a 1937 Mercury Theatre Julius Caesar in which Cinna the Poet is killed by fascist secret police
Director: directed the original 1937 production of Marc Blitzstein's The Cradle Will Rock, which was shut down by the federal government on opening night
Film: docudrama about art forger Elmyr de Hory and Clifford Irving's dubious autobiography of Howard Hughes; Welles's partner Oja Kodar also appears
work: Other Films (Mr. Arkadin, F for Fake, Chimes at Midnight, The Trial)

front: Film: smuggler Guy Van Stratten is hired to research the title millionaire's past, of which he has no memory before 1927
work: Other Films (Mr. Arkadin, F for Fake, Chimes at Midnight, The Trial)

front: Film: Kafka adaptation with Romy Schneider as Leni, made during Welles's European period
work: Other Films (Mr. Arkadin, F for Fake, Chimes at Midnight, The Trial)
